channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: 'It's a shame': One of the capital's favourite guinguettes forced to close due to vandalism
item.description: "These are acts of damage around the site, including cable thefts that affect the operation of the guinguette," said Guinguettes Barc manager François de Villers.

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 11 to 12 July
item.description: Brussels is hotting up again, so this week, we checked out some cool places where your kids can play away from the heat.

item.title: Professional card revamp: Brussels to update freelance work permit process for non-EU citizens
item.description: From early 2027, non‑EU freelance work permit applications will move to the federal Working in Belgium portal. What changes can you expect to see?

item.title: Person hospitalised and traffic disrupted after fire at recycling plant in north Brussels
item.description: Although the fire is now under control, firefighters are continuing to tackle remaining hotspots.

item.title: Three people injured after being hit by a car in Molenbeek
item.description: One of the victims is in a critical condition.

item.title: Residents told to close windows as huge fire breaks out at recycling plant in north Brussels
item.description: The blaze started in a 2,500-square-metre warehouse on Chaussée de Buda.

item.title: Brussels' new digital metro totems win design award
item.description: The new digital information signs at metro stations in Brussels won a Red Dot Award for Product Design.

item.title: Old Brussels motorway transformed into green urban boulevard
item.description: The redesigned stretch of the motorway will allow locals to stroll or cycle along what was once a busy and noisy main access road into the capital.

item.title: Woluwe-Saint-Lambert secures settlement on Brussels Metro noise nuisances
item.description: The Brussels municipality has welcomed temporary measures by the Brussels public transport operator STIB to reduce noise and vibrations from the metro.

item.title: Fire at Kanal Museum: New architecture centre will not be ready for grand opening
item.description: "It is a huge blow at this final stage before opening," said director Yves Goldstein, with the museum's official opening finally due in November.

item.title: Surprise STIB strike in Brussels on Monday – but are more expected?
item.description: On Monday, an unexpected strike action on Brussels' public transport network, STIB/MIVB, caught many commuters unaware.

item.title: 'For Flemish people': Flanders considers restricting Dutch-language education in Brussels
item.description: "With all due respect, Dutch-language education in Brussels is primarily intended for the Flemish people living in Brussels."

item.title: 'Shocking lack of forethought': Saint-Gilles transport chaos sparks anger among residents
item.description: "They have announced four tram closures, and they are just expecting people to accept this until April 2027? That's unreasonable and shows a shocking lack of forethought."

item.title: Fire at the future Kanal Museum in Brussels brought under control
item.description: A fire broke out late on Monday at the construction site of the future Kanal–Centre Pompidou museum near Place Sainctelette in Brussels.

item.title: STIB donates 36 Brussels buses to Ukraine
item.description: STIB is disposing of the buses as part of the transition to an electric fleet.

item.title: Union Saint-Gilloise's new stadium permit approved but with conditions
item.description: The Brussels football team has received conditional permission to build a new stadium in Forest, but are still being asked to address additional questions.

item.title: Belgium v US: Where to watch the Red Devils in Brussels?
item.description: Belgium will face the United States in the World Cup at 02:00 local time tonight – so where can you watch the game in Brussels?

item.title: Brussels' Cinema Palace evacuated following smoke caused by car doing doughnuts
item.description: The interior was filled with smoke and the smell of burning.

item.title: Strike disrupts public transport in Brussels: Which lines are running?
item.description: Due to picket lines, the number of services running is limited, as drivers are unable to leave the depot.

item.title: Feeling lonely in Brussels? Grab an empty chair
item.description: "I want people to know that there will always be someone sitting in a café with an empty chair for them."
